Defendant Bb/Cc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Declaratory Relief And Clarification Of January 26, 2023 Order Staying Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Gregory MacDonald, Esq., CLARK HILL LLP (415) 984-8500 GMacDonald@ClarkHill.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff, BLOCK 6 JOINT VENTURE LLC,
Tom Fama, Esq., WOOD, SMITH, HENNING & BERMAN LLP 925 222 3400 Attorneys for Defendants BALFOUR BEATTY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC., et al

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BALFOUR BEATTY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'s Motion For Declaratory Relief And Clarification Of January 26, 2023 Order Staying Proceedings

Defendant BB/CC's motion for declaratory relief and clarification of January 26, 2023 order staying proceedings" is denied.

A focus of this construction litigation is whether Uponor, Inc. PEX plumbing pipes in an apartment building are defective. That is among issues being resolved in an arbitration between some of the same parties. Thus, the court granted a defense motion to stay this court case pending completion of the arbitration.

No "declaratory relief" is identified in BB/CC's notice of motion. Moreover, if "clarification" of the court's order were necessary, it would have been sought nine months ago, when the order was entered.

BB/CC acknowledges that defense counsel and experts were permitted to examine the apartment building before issuance of the stay. (Fama Dec. 2:25-28.) And plaintiff states that it continues "photographing, documenting, storing, and replacing the PEX throughout the building." (Opp. 2:2-4.) Should any spoliation issues nonetheless arise, defendants will have their remedies.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
